curriculum.name: 10X EMPLOYEE Week 1: Program Overview
curriculum.elementDesc: 10X EMPLOYEE for Intrapreneurs is an individual self-improvement program for employees who are looking to contribute to their organizations at 10X levels. The content is based upon Brandon Dawson’s multiple decades of private and corporate business ownership and his journey of discovery on how to effectively prepare for scaling a business, while bringing along his team of employees who aligned with his mission, vision, and values and helped contribute to the growth of the organizations.

10X EMPLOYEE Week 2: Belief & Visualization
curriculum.elementDesc: Your belief lid is established by your level of success within your environment and the people you have surrounded yourself with each day. In order to break through that current level to elevate your belief lid, you have to challenge yourself to think and believe differently. This is done through the awareness of the relationship between your conscious and subconscious mind.

curriculum.elementDesc: Goals should be established in order to help structure your thinking, leading to what you say, and ultimately what you do. It's an essential and powerful component when it comes to identifying and accomplishing significant milestones in your life.

10X EMPLOYEE Week 4: Finding Mentors
curriculum.elementDesc: You’re the average of the five people you spend the most time with. In order to grow in anyway, you need to pay close attention to who you’re spending time around and to whom you’re giving your most energy. Identify who’s influencing you most closely and determine how they influence your overall personal, professional and financial goals.

10X EMPLOYEE Week 14: The Confidence Meter
curriculum.elementDesc: The Confidence Meter is designed to help set and correct expectations with an individual in regard to their overall contributions. It provides a simple structure for having an effective conversation in the event that mistakes are made, which creates the space for a team member to see the direct correlation between the type of mistake being made and the negative impact it has on the organization.
